title: Riot Platforms: Pivoting to HPC Leadership and Corsicana Catalyst for Value Creation
text: Riot Platforms acquired two additional parcels of land in 2025 for its Corsicana campus in Texas—355 acres in May, followed by another 238 acres in July, bringing the total size of the Corsicana data center campus to 858 acres. The company is allocating a majority of the site's power to high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI) workloads, with a total of 600MW expected to be allocated to HPC and AI workloads. Riot is aggressively pushing into AI and HPC data center development at its Corsicana facility, with the company having secured 1 GW of power capacity—400 MW currently operational and a 600 MW substation expansion planned for completion in 2026. In Q3 2025, the company initiated core and shell development for the first two buildings, which will represent 112 MW of critical IT load capacity. Proximity to Dallas and robust fiber networks ensure sub-5ms latency to hubs like New York and Silicon Valley. Riot believes mixed use of the facility has potential to maximize value for shareholders by providing long-term, predictable cash flows, recognizing a significant opportunity to maximize the value of the 600 MW of remaining capacity available at the Corsicana Facility.
